opportunity.title: Jeriko – an AI agent that runs directly inside your OS
opportunity.why: User looking for AI agent/tool — kbot could be relevant
opportunity.action: REVIEW: Consider replying with kbot if genuinely helpful

actions.draft: Native execution removes process boundaries, making context window management a real problem - you can't just fork workers and expect isolation without implementing your own checkpointing. I've found that tool calling patterns fail when you strip away sandboxing; you need explicit permission layers for system calls rather than relying on environment variables.
actions.reasoning: I have hands-on experience building multi-provider AI agents with tool orchestration and have considered how execution environments affect agent reliability and performance.
